recipes.title: Classic PB&J Sandwich
recipes.description: A first-kitchen sandwich that teaches spreading, slicing, cleanup, and making food with care.
ingredients: 2 tbsp peanut butter or sunflower butter
instructions: Wash hands and wipe the counter.
Lay both slices of bread on a plate.
Spread peanut butter on one slice and jelly on the other.
Add banana slices if using.
Close the sandwich, cut with grown-up help if needed, and wipe up sticky spots.
tips: Use sunflower butter for a nut-free version.
Spread all the way to the edges so every bite tastes good.
Try strawberry, grape, peach, or apple jelly.
recipes.makeAhead: Assemble the sandwich the same day so the bread stays soft.
servingIdeas: Cut into triangles or small squares.
Add a glass of milk or water.
recipes.chefNotes: A true first recipe: safe, familiar, and full of tiny skills kids can master.
recipes.cooking_temperature: Chill drinks thoroughly before serving unless the recipe is served warm.
recipes.internal_temperature: No internal temperature needed.
common_mistakes: Adding ice too early and watering down the pitcher.
Not tasting for sweetness and acid balance.
Serving without enough ice for a long gathering.
troubleshooting: If too sweet, add cold water or citrus.
If flat, add lemon, mint, or a pinch of salt depending on the drink.
If serving outside, keep pitchers cold in ice.
recipes.storage: Refrigerate covered up to 3 to 4 days depending on fresh fruit or dairy.

recipes.title: Easy Stovetop Mac and Cheese
recipes.description: Creamy mac and cheese for practicing measuring, stirring, draining, and melting cheese with grown-up help.
instructions: Boil macaroni in salted water with grown-up help.
Drain pasta carefully.
Return pasta to the warm pot on low heat.
Stir in butter, milk, and cheese until creamy.
Taste and add a tiny pinch of salt or pepper if needed.
tips: Keep the heat low after draining so the cheese melts without getting grainy.
Add a splash more milk if the sauce gets too thick.
Let kids measure and stir while grown-ups handle boiling water.
recipes.makeAhead: Best eaten fresh, but leftovers can be warmed with a splash of milk.
servingIdeas: Serve with fruit or steamed broccoli.
Make it a small lunch bowl.
recipes.chefNotes: This one teaches patience: low heat, steady stirring, and tasting before serving.
recipes.cooking_temperature: Use the heat level in the steps and adjust gently so food cooks evenly without scorching.

If it cooks too quickly, lower the heat and give it time.
If texture is off, check moisture, heat, and cook time before changing ingredients.

recipes.reheating: Reheat gently until hot throughout; leftovers with meat or dairy should reach 165 F.

instructions: Crack eggs into a bowl with grown-up help.

Stir slowly instead of rushing.
Take the pan off the heat early because eggs keep cooking.

recipes.title: Orange Chicken
recipes.description: Crispy chicken bites tossed in a sweet orange glaze that kids can help mix.
ingredients: 1 1/2 lb chicken breast, cut into bite-size pieces
Green onion or sesame seeds, optional
instructions: Pat chicken dry, then season with salt and pepper.
Toss chicken pieces with cornstarch until lightly coated on every side.
Pan-cook chicken in a thin layer of oil until golden and cooked through, working in batches if needed.
Simmer orange juice, soy sauce, honey, garlic, and ginger until glossy and slightly reduced.
Toss chicken in the sauce until every piece is coated.
Serve over warm rice and finish with green onion or sesame seeds.
tips: Dry chicken browns better than wet chicken.
Keep the pan from crowding so the coating gets crisp instead of steaming.
For a spicier version, add chili flakes or a spoon of chili garlic sauce to the glaze.
recipes.makeAhead: Cut the chicken and mix the sauce up to one day ahead. Cook the chicken fresh so the coating stays crisp.
servingIdeas: Make orange chicken bowls with cucumber, carrots, and green onion.
recipes.chefNotes: A Shay's Kitchen favorite because it feels fun, familiar, and easy to build confidence with.
recipes.cooking_temperature: Use the recipe heat setting; avoid crowding so food cooks evenly.
recipes.internal_temperature: Poultry should reach 165 F in the thickest part.
common_mistakes: Crowding the pan.
Cooking by color only instead of temperature.
Skipping rest time when the recipe calls for it.
troubleshooting: If browning too fast, lower heat and finish gently.
If dry, slice thinner and serve with sauce or pan juices.
If underdone, return to heat until 165 F.
recipes.storage: Cool within 2 hours; refrigerate up to 3 to 4 days.

recipes.title: Crab Rangoon
recipes.description: Crispy wontons filled with creamy crab, scallions, and a little sweetness.
ingredients: 1 cup crab meat or imitation crab, chopped
Small bowl of water, for sealing
instructions: Stir cream cheese, crab, green onion, garlic powder, Worcestershire, and sugar until evenly mixed.
Place one spoonful of filling in the center of each wonton wrapper.
Wet the wrapper edges with water and pinch closed tightly so the filling stays inside.
Fry, bake, or air-fry until the wrappers are crisp and golden.
Let cool for a minute, then serve with sweet chili sauce.
equipment: Air fryer, oven, or heavy pot for frying
tips: Do not overfill the wrappers or they may split.
Keep unused wrappers covered with a towel so they do not dry out.
If air-frying, lightly spray the outside for better browning.
recipes.makeAhead: Mix the filling a day ahead. Assemble right before cooking, or freeze assembled rangoons in a single layer.
servingIdeas: Pair with fried rice or orange chicken.
Add to a party board with dipping sauces.
recipes.chefNotes: Great for game nights, grazing tables, and a starter that feels special without being fussy.
recipes.cooking_temperature: Use steady medium to medium-high heat so seafood cooks quickly without drying out.
recipes.internal_temperature: Fish should reach 145 F or turn opaque and flake easily; shrimp should turn opaque and firm.

Starting with wet seafood when searing or frying.
Leaving cooked seafood out too long.
troubleshooting: If seafood releases water, pat it dry next time and use higher heat.
If overcooked, serve with sauce or lemon to help the texture.
If underdone, return to gentle heat briefly.
recipes.storage: Cool within 2 hours; refrigerate up to 3 days.

recipes.title: Yakamein
recipes.description: A New Orleans-style beef noodle bowl with seasoned broth, tender beef, spaghetti, boiled egg, and green onion.
ingredients: 1 lb beef chuck or stew meat
instructions: Season beef with Creole seasoning and garlic powder.
Simmer beef in broth with soy sauce until tender and easy to pull apart.
Boil spaghetti separately so the noodles stay springy.
Taste broth and adjust with hot sauce, salt, or lemon.
Build bowls with noodles, broth, beef, boiled egg, and green onion.
Serve hot with extra hot sauce on the side.
tips: Cook noodles separately so leftovers do not soak up all the broth.
A splash of lemon or pickle juice can brighten the bowl.
Use leftover roast beef when you need a faster version.
recipes.makeAhead: Broth and beef can be made two days ahead. Store noodles separately and assemble bowls when ready to eat.
servingIdeas: Serve as a late-night comfort bowl.
Add extra green onion and hot sauce for a New Orleans-style finish.

recipes.title: Braised Beef Chilli
recipes.description: A Mexican recipe imported from TheMealDB and organized for the Let's Cook Y'all kitchen.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 120C/225F/gas mark 1.
Take the meat out of the fridge to de-chill.
Pulse the onions and garlic in a food processor until finely chopped.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large casserole and sear the meat on all sides until golden.
Set to one side and add another small slug of oil to brown the chorizo.
Remove and add the onion and garlic, spices, herbs and chillies then cook until soft in the chorizo oil.
Season with salt and pepper and add the vinegar, tomatoes, ketchup and sugar.
Put all the meat back into the pot with 400ml water (or red wine if you prefer), bring up to a simmer and cook, covered, in the low oven.
After 2 hours, check the meat and add the beans.
Cook for a further hour and just before serving, pull the meat apart with a pair of forks.

recipes.title: Cajun spiced fish tacos
recipes.description: A Mexican recipe imported from TheMealDB and organized for the Let's Cook Y'all kitchen.
instructions: Cooking in a cajun spice and cayenne pepper marinade makes this fish super succulent and flavoursome.
Top with a zesty dressing and serve in a tortilla for a quick, fuss-free main that's delightfully summery.
On a large plate, mix the cajun spice and cayenne pepper with a little seasoning and use to coat the fish all over.
Heat a little oil in a frying pan, add in the fish and cook over a medium heat until golden.
Reduce the heat and continue frying until the fish is cooked through, about 10 minutes.
Cook in batches if you don’t have enough room in the pan.
Meanwhile, prepare the dressing by combining all the ingredients with a little seasoning.
Soften the tortillas by heating in the microwave for 5-10 seconds.
Pile high with the avocado, lettuce and spring onion, add a spoonful of salsa, top with large flakes of fish and drizzle over the dressing.

recipes.title: Chicken Enchilada Casserole
recipes.description: A Mexican recipe imported from TheMealDB and organized for the Let's Cook Y'all kitchen.
instructions: Cut each chicken breast in about 3 pieces, so that it cooks faster and put it in a small pot.
Pour Enchilada sauce over it and cook covered on low to medium heat until chicken is cooked through, about 20 minutes.
No water is needed, the chicken will cook in the Enchilada sauce.
Make sure you stir occasionally so that it doesn't stick to the bottom.
Remove chicken from the pot and shred with two forks.
Preheat oven to 375 F degrees.
Start layering the casserole.
Start with about ¼ cup of the leftover Enchilada sauce over the bottom of a baking dish.
I used a longer baking dish, so that I can put 2 corn tortillas across.
Place 2 tortillas on the bottom, top with ⅓ of the chicken and ⅓ of the remaining sauce.
Sprinkle with ⅓ of the cheese and repeat starting with 2 more tortillas, then chicken, sauce, cheese.
Repeat with last layer with the remaining ingredients, tortillas, chicken, sauce and cheese.

recipes.title: Brown Stew Chicken
recipes.description: A Jamaican recipe imported from TheMealDB and organized for the Let's Cook Y'all kitchen.
instructions: Squeeze lime over chicken and rub well.
Drain off excess lime juice.
Combine tomato, scallion, onion, garlic, pepper, thyme, pimento and soy sauce in a large bowl with the chicken pieces.
Cover and marinate at least one hour.
Heat oil in a dutch pot or large saucepan.
Shake off the seasonings as you remove each piece of chicken from the marinade.
Reserve the marinade for sauce.
Lightly brown the chicken a few pieces at a time in very hot oil.
Place browned chicken pieces on a plate to rest while you brown the remaining pieces.
Drain off excess oil and return the chicken to the pan.
Pour the marinade over the chicken and add the carrots.
Stir and cook over medium heat for 10 minutes.

recipes.title: Callaloo and SaltFish
recipes.description: A Jamaican recipe imported from TheMealDB and organized for the Let's Cook Y'all kitchen.
instructions: Soak salted fish in water overnight.
Next, heat salted fish in water on stove until water boils.
You should see a foam on top.
Remove from heat and drain.
Set aside and shred salted fish once it cools.
Cook bacon in skillet over medium heat until crispy.
Remove bacon from heat and drain the majority of the bacon grease, leaving about 1 tablespoon in the skillet.
Add yellow onion, green onion, scotch bonnet pepper, and garlic to the skillet and stir.
Cook for about 2 minutes or until onions soften.
Add salted fish to skillet and stir.
Cook for about a minute.
Next, add callaloo, roma tomatoes, thyme, and black pepper.

recipes.title: Chicken Alfredo Primavera
recipes.description: An Italian recipe imported from TheMealDB and organized for the Let's Cook Y'all kitchen.
instructions: Heat 1 tablespoon of butter and 2 tablespoons of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Season both sides of each chicken breast with seasoned salt and a pinch of pepper.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook for 5-7 minutes on each side, or until cooked through.
While the chicken is cooking, bring a large pot of water to a boil.
Season the boiling water with a few generous pinches of kosher salt.
Add the pasta and give it a stir.
Cook, stirring occasionally, until al dente, about 12 minutes.
Reserve 1/2 cup of  pasta water before draining the pasta.
Remove the chicken from the pan and transfer it to a cutting board; allow it to rest.
Turn the heat down to medium and dd the remaining 1 tablespoon of butter and olive oil to the same pan you used to cook the chicken.
Add the veggies (minus the garlic) and red pepper flakes to the pan and stir to coat with the oil and butter (refrain from seasoning with salt until the veggies are finished browning).
Cook, stirring often, until the veggies are tender, about 5 minutes.

recipes.title: Gigantes Plaki
recipes.description: A Greek recipe imported from TheMealDB and organized for the Let's Cook Y'all kitchen.
instructions: Soak the beans overnight in plenty of water.
Drain, rinse, then place in a pan covered with water.
Bring to the boil, reduce the heat, then simmer for approx 50 mins until slightly tender but not soft.
Drain, then set aside.
Heat oven to 180C/160C fan/gas 4.
Heat the olive oil in a large frying pan, tip in the onion and garlic, then cook over a medium heat for 10 mins until softened but not browned.
Add the tomato purée, cook for a further min, add remaining ingredients, then simmer for 2-3 mins.
Season generously, then stir in the beans.
Tip into a large ovenproof dish, then bake for approximately 1 hr, uncovered and without stirring, until the beans are tender.
The beans will absorb all the fabulous flavours and the sauce will thicken.
Allow to cool, then scatter with parsley and drizzle with a little more olive oil to serve.

recipes.title: Air Fryer Egg Rolls
recipes.description: A Chinese recipe imported from TheMealDB and organized for the Let's Cook Y'all kitchen.
instructions: Alternative Pan Fry Method: If you don’t have access to an air fryer, you can make these egg rolls using a traditional pan fry method.
Add enough oil to a medium skillet with high walls so the oil is about 1/2 inch up the side of the skillet.
Heat oil on medium high heat until it reaches 350°F.
Add egg rolls and fry for 3 to 4 minutes, flip, and fry for another 3 to 4 minutes until golden brown.
Remove and let them drain and cool on a few paper towels.
Cook the filling:
In a large skillet over medium heat, add the olive oil along with the ground pork or chicken.
Break apart the meat with a spatula or wooden spoon as it cooks.
Cook until the meat is cooked through, 6 to 8 minutes.
Add garlic, ginger, carrot, scallions, and cabbage.
Continue to cook until cabbage wilts down and is soft, another 3 to 4 minutes, stirring regularly.
Season the filling with soy sauce and rice wine vinegar, and take off the heat to cool. (This filling can be made in advance.)

recipes.title: Chicken Karaage
recipes.description: A Japanese recipe imported from TheMealDB and organized for the Let's Cook Y'all kitchen.
instructions: Add the ginger, garlic, soy sauce, sake and sugar to a bowl and whisk to combine.
Add the chicken, then stir to coat evenly.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
Add 1 inch of vegetable oil to a heavy bottomed pot and heat until the oil reaches 360 degrees F.
Line a wire rack with 2 sheets of paper towels and get your tongs out.
Put the potato starch in a bowl
Add a handful of chicken to the potato starch and toss to coat each piece evenly.
Fry the karaage in batches until the exterior is a medium brown and the chicken is cooked through.
Transfer the fried chicken to the paper towel lined rack.
If you want the karaage to stay crispy longer, you can fry the chicken a second time, until it's a darker color after it's cooled off once.
Serve with lemon wedges.

recipes.title: Japanese gohan rice
recipes.description: A Japanese recipe imported from TheMealDB and organized for the Let's Cook Y'all kitchen.
instructions: STEP 1
Rinsing and soaking your rice is key to achieving the perfect texture.
Measure the rice into a bowl, cover with cold water, then use your fingers to massage the grains of rice – the water will become cloudy.
Drain and rinse again with fresh water.
Repeat five more times until the water stays clear.
STEP 2

Tip the rinsed rice into a saucepan with 400ml water, or 200ml dashi and 200ml water, bring to the boil, then turn down the heat to a low simmer, cover with a tight-fitting lid with a steam hole and cook for 15 mins.
Remove from the heat and leave to sit for another 15 mins, then stir through the mirin.
Remove the lid and give it a good stir.
Serve with any or all of the optional toppings.

recipes.title: Southern Fried Chicken
recipes.description: Crisp, deeply seasoned fried chicken with a buttermilk soak, sturdy flour coating, and juicy meat inside.
instructions: Pat chicken dry and season lightly with salt and pepper.
Whisk buttermilk and hot sauce, add chicken, and marinate at least 30 minutes or overnight.
Combine flour, cornstarch, seasoned salt, paprika, garlic powder, and pepper in a shallow pan.
Heat oil in a heavy pot to 325-350 F.
Dredge chicken firmly in the flour mix, rest 10 minutes, then fry in batches until golden and 165 F inside.
Drain on a rack, not paper towels, so the crust stays crisp.
tips: Let dredged chicken rest before frying so the crust sticks.
Do not overcrowd the pot.

recipes.title: Hot Water Cornbread
recipes.description: Crisp-edged cornmeal patties mixed with boiling water and fried until golden, a classic Southern fish fry and greens-table bread.
instructions: Whisk cornmeal, salt, and sugar in a heat-safe bowl.

Fold in onion if using and let the mixture rest 5 minutes so the cornmeal hydrates.
Heat 1/2 inch oil in a cast iron skillet over medium heat until shimmering.
Shape small patties with wet hands and fry 2 to 3 minutes per side until deep golden.
Drain on a rack, sprinkle lightly with salt, and serve hot with greens, catfish, or beans.

recipes.title: Southern Caramel Cake
recipes.description: Tender yellow cake layers covered with cooked caramel icing for holidays, church tables, and family celebrations.
instructions: Heat oven to 350 F and grease two 9-inch cake pans.
Whisk flour, baking powder, and salt.
Cream butter and sugar until fluffy, then beat in eggs one at a time.
Alternate dry ingredients and milk into the batter; stir in vanilla.
Bake 28 to 35 minutes until the layers spring back, then cool completely.
For icing, simmer brown sugar, evaporated milk, and butter 3 to 4 minutes.
Beat in powdered sugar until spreadable, then frost the cake while icing is warm.

recipes.description: Peppery, low-and-slow beef ribs smoked until tender with a bark that tastes like Texas barbecue.

Rub ribs with binder, then season with salt, pepper, and garlic.
Smoke at 250 F bone-side down.

Cook until probe-tender around 200 to 205 F internal.
Rest 45 minutes before slicing between bones.
